reviews.title: Chaotic check-in and poor room conditions
reviews.text:
Positive - location as it is a stone throw to the main shopping avenue of Shanghai and a 20 minutes walk to the Bund and Shanghai museum.  The MRT is also a short walk away.  Spoil for choice of well known eateries in nearby.

Negatives - the check-in was chaotic.  Despite checking in at 3pm, was told that twin rooms are not available yet, and was told to come back later.  When I returned after lunch at 5pm, was told the room was still not ready and the hotel was unsure when a room will be available.  We pointed out that the check in time has way passed, and finally they upgraded us to an executive floor twin room.

The room left much to be happy about - certain spots on the floor was sticky and had a layer of dust; the shelves in the room also had a layer of dust, and the bed side light switches on the left bed were not working and loose.  Further more, the reading light was not working.  The bathroom floor was covered in a layer of dirt.  We told the front desk to ask that the floor be cleaned and to repair the switches.  However, only the floor was cleaned.

Overall, the room failed to meet my expectation of a Conrad brand.  I believe for the same rate, one can get a better hotel in the area.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
I stayed at Conrad Shanghai and had a mixed experience overall.

Upon arrival, I checked in around midnight and the hotel was not busy. However, no staff offered to assist with my luggage despite several staff walking around the lobby. This was quite disappointing for a hotel of this standard.

During my stay, I ordered in-room dining one night. The staff who delivered the food appeared unhappy and unwilling to serve. There was no greeting or smile. He simply asked to come in and place the food down, which felt quite cold and unwelcoming.

The concierge service was also lacking. I had difficulty using the Meituan locker for my parcel, and no one stepped in to assist. Eventually, my parcel was left at the concierge counter, and the staff casually mentioned it was probably too big for the locker. There was no initiative to help further.

I also ordered groceries such as bottled water and snacks. As the items were heavy, I had to make three trips to bring everything up myself. Again, no staff offered assistance, which was surprising. I would have gladly tipped if help had been offered.

That said, my experience improved on the last day. The check-out staff, a lady, was very pleasant and proactively helped push my luggage to the lift. Another male staff also assisted by bringing my luggage to the entrance and even helped communicate with my airport driver. This was the only part of the service that felt truly warm and professional.

Room-wise, I booked a Premium King room. The room was spacious and well-designed, with a separate living area, large sofa, writing table, coffee table, and two TVs (one in the living area and one by the bed). The full-length windows offered an amazing view from level 39.

I had requested child amenities in advance, and these were prepared and provided upon check-in, which was appreciated.

The bed was slightly firm. I called housekeeping to request different pillow options as stated on the in-room leaflet, but was told only cotton pillows were available.

WiFi connectivity was also quite limited. It mainly worked for Chinese apps, and many Western apps were not accessible. It is better to rely on your own mobile data instead.

The location is excellent, just a 1–2 minute walk to the mall and Nanjing Pedestrian Road. Having Haidilao Hot Pot in the hotel (lvl 8) is a plus, although the toilet there was quite dirty.

Overall, while the room and location were great, the service was inconsistent and below expectations for a luxury hotel. With better staff attentiveness and service attitude, the experience would have been much better.

For the price and rating of the hotel, I expected a much smoother and more polished service throughout. The concierge team is lovely and are very helpful.

Unfortunately, compared to other 5 star hotels I’ve stayed at, this experience fell short and I wouldn't stay here again.

reviews.title: Conrad Shanghai~
reviews.text: I was contemplating which hotel to stay at for my first trip to Shanghai, and I thought Conrad would be a good choice, so I made a reservation. The location is perfect, near Nanjing East Road, which was incredibly convenient and nice. The area around the hotel is a shopping spot, making it great for buying souvenirs, and it connects all the way to the Bund along the road, making it very convenient! Additionally, it's very close to People's Park, which was great for a morning walk every day! And the breakfast, which is the start of the day... the menu is amazing! 👏🏻 I've never seen such a wide variety of options in any hotel before. Truly impressive, Conrad... I was happily full every morning... I think I will stay there again when I visit Shanghai next time!!! ☺️

reviews.text: The front desk staff I met were all responsive and proactive in solving problems, especially Mr. Laughing as the Assistant Manager of the Hotel. reviews.title: The location is convenient, and the night view is very beautiful.
reviews.text:
I used this hotel because the price was reasonable compared to the Conrad in Korea, and the location was good. When I went there, I found that there were a lot of Koreans who seemed to have similar thoughts as me.

What I found disappointing was that the front desk staff were not very fluent in English, and when checking in, they handed me the voucher and room key without any additional explanation.

I was also looking forward to the swimming pool, but I found a notice in the room that said the pool was under construction. Fortunately, the construction was completed during my stay, so I was able to visit the pool, but it was disappointing that the showers were not in operation.

The location was good, as it took about 25 minutes to walk from the Bund while enjoying the sights, and the hotel was easily visible like Sauron's tower, so I didn't have to worry about getting lost. There were many shops and restaurants in the area that were open late. There is also an exit to the subway station right behind the building (People's Square Station).

The room had a bathtub, so I enjoyed soaking in it, and since I chose a high-floor room, I had a great view of Shanghai's skyline!

When using delivery services, there is a dedicated locker for Meituan on the first floor outside the building, which made it convenient to receive items even if there was a language barrier. It might be a bit annoying that you have to switch elevators one or two times to get to the room, but the elevator turnover was relatively quick, so the wait time was not long.

Overall, it was a comfortable and convenient stay, and I would consider staying here again when visiting Shanghai.

reviews.title: The location is very good, the room is very comfortable, and the view is beautiful.
reviews.text:
The hotel is in a prime location, surrounded by shops and malls where you can buy anything. Within a 10-minute walk, you can enjoy Michelin's crab meat soup dumplings. There are many coffee shops nearby; since I'm on a business trip, coffee is very important to me. There is a massage parlor across from the hotel (across from the police station) that is very comfortable and thoughtful, I go there for a massage every evening.

The subway is very close. The hotel staff is also very helpful and polite.

The room is smaller than I expected, but it is still very adequate. The double room does not have a higher floor or a sea view, but the park view is also lovely.

The bathroom is well spaced, so while my friend is showering, others can still use the toilet.

reviews.title: The best cost performance.
reviews.text:
I stayed with my mother for three nights during the Lunar New Year holiday. Considering that it's a Hilton Group hotel, the price for a night with breakfast included is in the mid-200,000 won range, which seems like a very good value.

The location is at the entrance of Nanjing East Road, so it's close to People's Square Station. It was less than a 5-minute walk. There is a shopping mall below the hotel where we also had some nice meals, and we enjoyed coffee at the See Saw cafe on the first floor. The staff at See Saw are really friendly...

However, one slight disappointment was the bathroom.. The water pressure of the shower was weak... and the space for toiletries was not sufficient, so when I hung up towels, there was no proper place to hang or put my clothes. It would be nice if there was at least a basket provided.

The room had a capsule coffee machine, and I enjoyed making my coffee every day. If I come again, I would be willing to stay here once more.

reviews.title: ５つ星にしては残念
reviews.text:
まず部屋について。部屋に敷いてある絨毯が何故かベタベタ。水をこぼしたように部分的にスリッパが湿るほど濡れていた。
スタッフについて。服の着方がだらしなく、高級ホテルのスタッフとは思えない。挨拶もしない。
その他。自国民の客が多いようだったが、スリッパやパジャマのまま朝食を食べていたり、子供が走り回っていたりして、あまり良い気はしなかった。

全体として。５つ星のクオリティは無かったと思う。目にするものがあまり気分の良いものでは無かった。

reviews.text:
The hotel and the room is amazing following high international standards, very clean and comfortable. The hotel is located very close to peoples square station.

The only bad experiences were that the room smelled so much like cigarettes all the time even when I requested non smoking room, and the front desk staff and reservation staff have very limited English communication skill given it's a Conrad hotel.  They were very nice nonetheless.

reviews.title: Overall, our stay was wonderful! Next time we are planning a trip to Shanghai we will definitely stay at Conrad Hotel!
reviews.positives: The location was great, staff were nice. The breakfast was so delicious. The room was clean and the bathroom was clean as well. Everything in the room was working very well. The staff were very helpful! Staff were very helpful with transfer! We really appreciate Julia, Colin and other staff!
reviews.negatives: We came to Shanghai very early in the morning, and the check-in was as 15:00, the hotel gave as the room at around 15:30, we were okay and understanding with it since there were a lot of people. However they said that there will be an upgrade for us, when we came to the upgraded room, it really didn’t seem like an upgrade. The room interior was dark ( we booked a nice bright room), and the smell was not good. But we talked with a staff and they gave us a nice bigger room! So it was very nice. Other than that we have no complaints.
